mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals persistent consumer demand strength that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines below 5.5%, it signals labor market tightness that supports wage-driven consumer spending and corporate pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 5.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production indicate coordinated demand-supply expansion that reduces recession probability and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 during months when both PAYEMS and INDPRO increase over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero lower bound period, a month-over-month increase in the consumer price index signals rising inflation expectations that reduce the probability of prolonged near-zero rates and trigger positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months when CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in monthly housing starts signals durable residential investment demand that drives construction employment and materials orders, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing activity and inventory destocking that reduces earnings estimates and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines two months in a row
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirm that labor income gains are translating directly into consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both rise in same month
failure_condition: Walk-forward hit rate of positive 5-day returns falls below 55% when both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase in the same month.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points over three months signals an abrupt monetary tightening that increases borrowing costs and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25pp over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25 percentage points over three months.
